items.title: Nothing confirms layoffs, but calls market exit rumors ‘fake news’
items.summary: In response to a report that Nothing is planning to "exit 12 markets as global shipments decline," Nothing cofounder Akis Evangelidis said the company is "reorganizing" and laying off some of its staff, but that "the reported numbers are way overblown." Evangelidis disputed a claim about underwhelming sales for Nothing's Phone 4B, saying it "sold […]

items.title: Exclusive: Score this new American leather MacBook backpack with built-in cable management at nearly 30% off
items.summary: Burton Goods, one of my personal favorite leather accessory makers, has unleashed its all-new (and particularly gorgeous), Modern Leather MacBook Backpack. Not only is there a limited time pre-order discount, but you can use our exclusive 9to520 code to knock an additional 20% off at checkout for the best deal you’ll find anywhere.

items.title: Why Cognition bought Poke: AI personality is becoming a competitive advantage
items.summary: AI coding startup Cognition has acquired Poke, the AI assistant you text like a friend, in a deal valuing the startup in the low nine figures. The acquisition brings Poke’s conversational style and interaction model to Cognition’s coding agent Devin, reflecting a growing belief that how AI assistants interact with users is as important as the models powering them.

items.title: Roku raises streaming hardware prices by up to $50
items.summary: Roku has increased prices across its streaming hardware, with the cheapest HD Streaming Stick now priced at $39.99 instead of $29.99, as first reported by The Desk. The price hike affects all of Roku's other devices as well, with some increasing by as much as $50. Here's a rundown of the changes: Roku is currently […]

Trump says the US will initiate a probe into the EU's practice of “robbing” US tech giants with fines, threatening the bloc with “substantial” tariffs  —  President Donald Trump on Friday said his administration will launch a trade probe into the European Union …

items.title: Don’t miss PNY’s 1TB Pro Elite SSD while it’s at one of its best prices since the hikes at $130
items.summary: We are now tracking a solid SSD deal at B&H that gets you PNY’s 1TB Pro Elite drive for $129.99 shipped. This drive was selling for closer to $75 before prices began climbing amid the ongoing memory shortage, and some third-party Amazon listings have recently pushed it as high as $170. Today’s deal delivers a notable discount on its usual going rate and marks one of the lowest prices we’ve tracked since the hikes began. It’s a pretty good option to consider at this price if you’re in the market for a new drive, especially now when the prices are continuing to trend upward. Head below for more details.

items.title: Letter: Qualcomm tells customers it plans to raise prices by double digit percentage points, after exhausting its ability to absorb higher costs from suppliers (Ian King/Bloomberg)

Letter: Qualcomm tells customers it plans to raise prices by double digit percentage points, after exhausting its ability to absorb higher costs from suppliers  —  Qualcomm Inc., the biggest maker of smartphone processors, plans to increase prices by a percentage in the double digits …

items.title: US accuses American of allegedly wiping his phone using a ‘duress’ password during border search
items.summary: A U.S. citizen has asked a court to throw out the government's claim that he gave over a passcode to border authorities that wiped his phone's data, opening up fresh questions about a person's constitutional rights at the U.S. border.

items.title: Anthropic expects Opus 5 "classifiers to intervene around 85% less often than they do for Fable 5"; Opus 5 is not included in its 30-day data retention policy (Igor Bonifacic/Engadget)
items.summary:

Anthropic expects Opus 5 “classifiers to intervene around 85% less often than they do for Fable 5”; Opus 5 is not included in its 30-day data retention policy  —  It also has less restrictive safeguards than the Fable model.  —  Just as it was starting to feel like Anthropic …

items.summary: Anduril is said to be raising a new round of funding that may push its valuation up to about $100 billion, per Reuters.

items.title: You can’t ignore Google Zero anymore
items.summary: The web and Google once had a deal: Google collects data and indexes webpages and in exchange sends oceans of traffic to websites. The deal wasn't perfect and certainly made Google more money than it made the websites, but it worked for a long time. Now, however, the deal seems to be dead. And the […]

items.title: Opus 5 improves coding, reasoning efficiency, and prompt-cache-friendly tool use, and is priced at $5/1M input tokens and $25/1M output, same as Opus 4.8 (David Gewirtz/ZDNET)
items.summary:
David Gewirtz / ZDNET:
Opus 5 improves coding, reasoning efficiency, and prompt-cache-friendly tool use, and is priced at $5/1M input tokens and $25/1M output, same as Opus 4.8  —  ZDNET's key takeaways  — Opus 5 targets coding, knowledge work, and agent workflows.  — Anthropic says it uses fewer tokens for similar quality.

items.title: Anthropic launches Claude Opus 5, which it says comes close to Fable 5 performance at half the price; it is the new default model on Claude Max (Anthropic)
items.summary:
Anthropic:
Anthropic launches Claude Opus 5, which it says comes close to Fable 5 performance at half the price; it is the new default model on Claude Max  —  Claude Opus 5 is available today.  It's a thoughtful and proactive model that comes close to the frontier intelligence of Claude Fable 5 at half the price.

Linkind Matter-Certified Stretchable Recessed Smart Bulbs (2-pack)

Buy for $30 (Reg. $40)

What we love

About 10 years ago I lived in a home with recessed lighting, and if I was still there, this sort of bulb would be on my list. Since they are stretchable, you can let more light out, which is really useful if you ask me. Matter certification means these will work with all the major players like Apple, Google, Alexa, and others.

You’ll be getting a $144 markdown here that even beats out its Black Friday 2025 pricing by $5, landing it at the best price we have tracked in 2026. Based on the way the price keeps fluctuating lately, if the deal disappears, be sure to check back in a day or two to see if it reappears.

items.title: Lectric’s Christmas in July Sale offers up to $733 FREE bundle savings + $100 price cuts on e-bikes starting from $799
items.summary: Lectric has switched to a higher gear with its newly launched Christmas in July Sale that is offering up to $733 savings and select $100 price cuts on e-bikes, as well as 30% off extra batteries. It’s a great opportunity to prep for upcoming events like heading off to school, with a top choice for students being the lightweight XP Lite2 Long-Range Folding Commuter e-bike with a FREE $429 bundle at $999 shipped, or its upgraded JW belt-drive counterpart with the same bundle at $1,099 shipped. These bundles are where the main savings are typically during a Lectric sale, which would cost you $1,428 or $1,528 for these models were that not the case. Aside from the $449 bundles we saw them getting back in February for Presidents’ Day, these are the biggest collections of gear we’ve seen on these in 2026, even beating the $346 Prime Day bundle deals. If you don’t care about the bundled gear, you can find the standard XP Lite2 e-bikes for $799 shipped, while the standard XP Lite2 JW Black Belt Drive model goes for $899 shipped. Head below to browse the full lineup of deals while they last.
